item.title: Activation of GABABR alleviates DSS-induced colitis in mice by rebalancing inflammatory responses and antioxidant capacity through IRAK-M.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - The pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease is complex, involving key contributors such as inflammatory factors and oxidative stress. While the gamma-aminobutyric acid B receptor (GABABR) is rec...

item.title: Genome-wide cross-trait analysis reveals shared genetic architecture between inflammatory bowel disease and ankylosing spondylitis.
item.description: Scientific reports - Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and ankylosing spondylitis (AS) are clinically closely related. They suffer from comorbidities, possibly attributed to a shared genetic structure. By ...

item.title: The shared genetic architecture between acne vulgaris and inflammatory bowel disease: a cross-trait analysis.
item.description: The Journal of investigative dermatology - Acne vulgaris (AV) is clinically associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet the biological mechanisms driving this association remain unclear. We investigated the shared genetic architec...

item.title: The gut-lung axis: pathological crosstalk and inter-organ communication in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - The significant bidirectional comorbidity risk and extensive subclinical involvement observed between chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) underscore th...

item.title: Associations of Circadian Clock Gene Variants with Clinical Features and Treatment Characteristics in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Growing evidence suggests that disruption of circadian rhythms contributes to the pathogenesis of inflammation and inflammatory bowel disease; however, clinical data linking ...
